name: Cumberland County Schools
about: Cumberland County Schools serves approximately 51,000 students in Fayetteville and Cumberland County, North Carolina. The district is home to many military families from nearby Fort Liberty (formerly Fort Bragg).
calendarNotes: Cumberland County Schools serves Fayetteville and the surrounding area adjacent to Fort Liberty (formerly Fort Bragg), one of the largest U.S. military installations. As a result, the district has one of the highest proportions of military family students in North Carolina, and staff are experienced with mid-year enrollment and records transfers during PCS (Permanent Change of Station) moves. The school year starts in late August and includes a Fall Break in October. Spring Break typically falls in mid-March. Summer PCS season — June through August — tends to bring a surge of new enrollment. Families PCSing to Fort Liberty should contact the district's school liaison office for streamlined enrollment assistance.
